text: CORK, PHILIP CLARKE.—Protector of immigrants, Grenada, 1881. Appointed clerk, immigration Department, Jamaica, 1875; Hindustani interpreter to the agent general of immigration, 1875; sub-agent of immigration of the 2nd class, 1876; inspector of immigrants of the 1st class, 1880.
BAILEY, N. L.—Chief Justice, Gold Coast, Oct., 1882.

HUGGINS, HASTINGS, C., LL.D.—Stipendiary magistrate at British Guiana; called to bar, Inner Temple, Jan. 1858; was Queen's counsel, solicitor-general, and speaker of the house of assembly, and member of the executive council at Nevis; and in 1861 completed a revised compilation of the laws of that island, from 1861 to 1861 inclusive; attorney-general of British Honduras, 1861; and became ex-officio member of the executive council.

HALKETT, CAPTAIN FRED., CRAIGIE.—1872, passed 14th of successful competitors for Royal Indian Engineering College; 1872, lieutenant 1st Royal Lanark Militia; 1873, first lieutenant Fife Artillery Militia; 1873, passed alone out of detachment, school of instruction, Woolwich; 1873, November, captain Fife Artillery Militia; 1876, August, artillery instructor; 1876, September, artillery inspector, Gold Coast, Hounsa Force; December, 1876, to May, 1877, acted as adjutant and inspector-general; 1876, November to May, 1878, served on Gold Coast in command of colonial artillery; 1877, March, on diplomatic mission to kings of Denkery and Warsaw, West Africa; 1877, April, commissioner, commandant, and treasurer of Dixcoo district; 1877, October, on special duty to Prabhu; 1878, January 4th to April 1st, on special duty to Ashanti frontier, inspecting roads and fortifications; 1878, April 19th, commissioner, commandant, and treasurer of Seepondee district; 1879, April 19th, stipendiary magistrate, Fiji; 1879, Jan., appointed a commissioner of supreme court.

SMITH, SIR JOHN SMALMAN, KT. BACH., 1896.—M.A., St. John's College, Cambridge; called to the bar by the Inner Temple, 1872; special pleader Oxford circuit; puisne judge, Gold Coast Colony, 1883; judge, Lagos, 1886; chief justice, Lagos, 1889; retired, 1895.

LOCKHART, J. H. STUART, F.R.G.S., M.R.A.S.—Edinburgh University; Greek medalist, &c.; appointed, after competitive examination, to a Hong Kong cadetship, 1878; attached to the Colonial Office for one year, 1879; passed cadet, 1882; superintendent, opium revenue, March, 1883; assistant colonial secretary and assistant auditor-general, Aug., 1883, acting registrar general, 1884 and 1885; reg.-gen., 1887, and M.L.C., 1889; member of ex-council, 1891; ag. col. secy., April, 1894; col. secy., and regr.-gen., 24th Mar., 1895.

BRACE, T. E. D.—Dispenser N. P. Asylum, Bahamas, Sep., 1882; clerk police office, Aug., 1883; 2nd clk. col. sec. offr., Sep., 1887; has acted on several occasions as chief clk. and clk. bd. of pub. wks.

HOCKING, SIR HENRY HICKS, K.T. BACH. (1895).—Educated at St. John's College, Oxford, where he graduated B.A. in 1864 and B.C.L. in 1867, 2nd class mods., 1st clas law and history; Vinerian scholar, 1866; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, Nov., 1867, and joined the Home circuit; attorney-general for Western Australia, 1872; acting chief justice, 1873; acting chief justice, Gibraltar, 1879; attorney-general, Jamaica, 1880; retired, 1896.
HOUGHTON, H. E.—B. 1892; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1912.
MANSKI, WILLIAM DU PRE.—Sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted police, 1893; asst. commr., 1905.

MEADE, A. R.—B. 1887; ed. at St. Kitts-Nevis gram. schl.; jun. ast. mast., St. Kitts-Nevis gram. schl., Jan. to Apr., 1907; matric., London Univ., 1908; copyist, treasy. dept., St. Kitts, June, 1907; 3rd treasy. clk., Montserrat, 16th Nov., 1907; ag. 1st treasy. clk., Dec. 1909 to Jan., 1910; ag. 2nd treasy. clk., Mar. to July, 1910; 2nd treasy. clk., July, 1910.
MARSHALL, FRANCIS.—B. So., Leeds Univ.; B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 17th Nov., 1911; attached to Galle Kachero, Dec., 1911.

KIRSPOTT, George Dougal.—Asst. collr. cust., E.A. Prot., Jan., 1914; collr., cust., Kenya Col., Jan., 1920; compt'r., cust., Zanzibar, Mar., 1923; ag. treas. for various periods, 1924, 1926, 1928, 1929.

DRAYSON, MATTHEW JOHN, I.S.O. (1926).—L. 1865; apptd., after compet. exam., a clerk in the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., May, 1883; asst. registr., May, 1892; promoted to higher grade 2nd div., July, 1896; seconded for service as chief clerk, col. sec. o.f.s., Hong Kong, 11th Jan., 1902; acted as clk. of coun., Aug., 1902; retld. to C.O. as dep. ch. registr., 22nd July, 1905; asst. acctnt., 27th Sept., 1915; clk. to commrs. of income tax, C.O., 1915-21; acctnt., 9th Aug., 1927; commr. for income tax, D.O. and C.O., 1927.

CLARKE, HON. SIR FRANCIS GRENVILLE, K.B.E., M.L.C.—B. 1879; ed. Scotch Coll., Melbourne, Melbourne Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; M.L.C. for N. Prov. (Vic.); min. for lands and water supply, Nov., 1917 to Oct., 1919; min. of pub. wks. and water supply, Oct., 1919 to July, 1923; pres., leg. coun., Victoria, July, 1923.

HANDS, ARTHUR L.C., M.B.E.—B. 1880; clk. in P.O., St. Helena, Sept., 1896; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s off., Apr., 1898; registr. of births and deaths, 1904-07; ch. clk. and clk. to exec. coun., Jan., 1907.
OLIVER, George Richard.—b. 1905; ed. Eastbourne Coll., Trinity Coll., Cambridge, M.A. (Cantab.); cadet, N. Rhod., 1927; dist. offr., 1929.

SLEEP, Arthur, B.Sc. (Man.).—b. 1894; ed. Ulverston Gram. Sch. and Manchester Univ.; on mil. serv. 1915-19, capt.; cadet, Mal. C.S., 1920; cl. V, 1922; cl. IV, 1926; cl. III, 1931; cl. II, 1936; cl. IB, 1938; dep. fin. sec., F.M.S.; ag. fin. comsnr., Johore; ag. fin. sec., S.S., cl. 1A; res. comsnr. Pahang (staff), 1946.
GARDNER-BROWN, Anthony Geoffrey Hopwood.—b. 1913; ed. Marlborough Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, B.A. (Cantab.); on mil. serv. 1940-43, lieut.; cadet, N. Rhod., 1936; dist. offr., 1938.

MACE, Norman, M.A., F.R.G.S.—b. 1906; ed. Wellingborough and Jesus, Cambridge, senr. opt. maths. trip.; on mil. serv. 1944-45, maj.; asst. survr., survey dept., Sarawak, 1928; asst. supt., lands and surveys, 1932; settlement offr., 1938; supt., lands and surveys, 1947; rep. for Br. Borneo at Lord Killearn's first food conf.; publications: Allied Geographical—Section SWPAC, Terrain Study, (89) Sarawak and Brunei, Terrain Study, (109) S.E. Borneo, Terrain Handbook, (59) Brunei Bay, and Special Report, (81) Kuching.

MORGAN, W. S.—b. 1908; ed. Rendcomb Coll., Cirencester, and Queens' Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1941-42; educ. offr., Mal., 1931; secon., C.O., 1943-46 and 1947; admin. offr., S.L., 1950; ch. asst. col. sec., 1953; sec., comsnr. on univ. educ., Mal.

BRIDGE, R. C.—b. 1910; ed. Charterhouse and Oxford Univ.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; mil. serv., 1940-46, wing cdr. (desps.); adv. and solr., Mal. and Sp'ore; C.L.S., 1947; regist., sup. ct., Mal., 1948; puisne judge, 1949; just. of appeal, E.A. ct. of appeal, 1953; chmn. Mal. armed local forces trib., 1949.
HUMPHREYS, F., O.B.E. (1955).—b. 1905; ed. Charterhouse Sch.; mem. Middle Temple; mil. serv., 1940-43, capt.; cadet, Nig., 1928; admin. offr., cl II, 1947; cl I, 1950.

BAFOUR, R. P., O.B.E. (1953)—b. 1912; ed. Mfantsipim Sch. and Achimota Coll.; asst. loco. supt., G.C. rwy., 1939; lectr., Achimota Coll., 1941; mech. engrnr., Accra munic., 1946; ch. trans. offr., 1953.
HARRAP, K. D.—b. 1919; ed. Danensis Sch.; mil. serv., 1939-49, maj.; labr. offr., Ken., 1950; Uga., 1954; senr. labr. offr., Ken., 1957; dep. comsnr. of labr., Fiji, 1962; comsnr., 1964.

REES, B.—b. 1937; ed. Willesden Tech. Coll., Prince of Wales Sch., Nairobi, and Royal Coll., Nairobi; mil. serv., 1956-57, Kenya Regt.; apprentice, E.A.R. & H., 1954; chargehand, 1960; working foreman, 1961; mech. foreman, 1963; senr. mech. foreman, 1964.
EDDY, B. M.—b. 1928; ed. Carlisle Gram. Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1947-49; cadet, N. Rhod., 1953; D.O., 1955; admin. offr., 1964. (Zambia Govt. service.)
CRAFFORD, G. P.—b. 1913; ed. Lichtenburg High Sch.; post and tel. asst., S.A., 1920; postmaster 1939; investn. offr., 1944-47; postmaster gr. IV, 1933; contr., posts and tels., Swaz., 1953-63.
